title: Treat model output as a claim, not a fact
description: Every value a model returns is an unverified assertion that must be checked against ground truth or a schema before code acts on it.
body: Use when wiring any model response into business logic, a database write, or a user-facing answer. Do not let a model's confident phrasing substitute for verification, and avoid treating extracted entities or computed numbers as correct just because they parsed. Actions: identify each model-derived value that drives a decision; verify it against a deterministic source (schema, lookup, recomputation) before use; record the raw response and the verified value separately. Validation signals include tests that feed plausible-but-wrong model output and assert the verification layer rejects it. Risk boundary: if a model value drives an irreversible action (payment, deletion, external send) with no independent check, escalate to a human gate.
